qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated localhost INTERNALCMD allows source-tree Haraka shutdown
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets localhost INTERNALCMD dispatch when config/internalcmd_key is absent, reaching connection.cmd_internalcmd -> server.receiveAsMaster -> performShutdown. The advisory instead describes remote email header parsing in node_modules/haraka-email-message/lib/header.js where a __proto__ header name causes _add_header to call push on Object.prototype and crash the worker via the uncaughtException exit path.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The only final qualifying finding is a different denial-of-service issue in Haraka. It depends on a localhost client invoking INTERNALCMD performShutdown in a source-tree deployment without config/internalcmd_key, not on the advisory's __proto__ mail-header parsing bug in header.js::_add_header.
evidence: The advisory summary and details identify node_modules/haraka-email-message/lib/header.js lines 215-218 as the vulnerable code, where _add_header stores attacker-controlled header names in a plain object and mishandles __proto__.
The advisory crash path is remote SMTP DATA processing of an email header name __proto__, ending in a TypeError that reaches haraka.js uncaughtException handling and exits the worker.

The session's final qualifying finding identifies connection.js:1207 and server.js:245, where a localhost INTERNALCMD request reaches Server.receiveAsMaster and performShutdown when config/internalcmd_key is absent.

The finding's own exploit path and PoC report mention SMTP INTERNALCMD performShutdown acknowledgement and shutdown logging, not any __proto__ header parsing, header.js::_add_header execution, or uncaught TypeError from Object.prototype.push.
